title: Mutex on full rate-limiter HashMap blocks all inbox requests
description:
LOCATION: src/ratelimit.rs:29-47

FINDING: try_acquire takes a Mutex<HashMap<String, Bucket>> for the entire duration of the token-bucket calculation. Every concurrent inbox request (both /inbox and /users/{u}/inbox) serializes through this single lock. On a busy server with many concurrent inbound federation requests, threads queue up waiting for the mutex even though per-IP state is independent.

IMPACT: Inbox throughput is artificially capped to single-threaded execution for the rate-limiter check. A DashMap (sharded concurrent hashmap) or per-key atomics would allow true parallelism. At the 60-req/min/IP limit with realistic federation traffic, contention is latent but will appear under load testing or during a follow storm from a large instance.
close_reason: Documented: ponytail comment on Mutex contention with DashMap upgrade path
